codeReview.summary: Most backend/web additions are coherent, but the Chrome-extension-to-intro flow is not wired end-to-end: the extension still posts to the generic contacts endpoint instead of the new extension capture endpoint. I also found a concrete URL-deduplication edge case in the new extension capture service.

issues.description: The live extension quick-capture path still calls `POST /api/contacts` (`path: '/api/contacts'`) rather than the newly introduced `POST /api/extension/capture`. This means the feature requirement/description path ('create contact via extension API endpoint -> view in web app -> request intro') is not actually integrated in the extension runtime flow. A repo-wide search under `packages/chrome-extension/src` finds no usage of `/api/extension/capture`.

LinkedIn URL dedupe claims trailing-slash-insensitive matching, but only normalizes the incoming URL (`replace(/\/+$/, '')`). The stored value side is only lowercased (`$toLower` on `$linkedInUrl`) and not slash-normalized, so existing `.../alice/` and incoming `.../alice` can fail to match and create duplicates.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `implementation-worker` skill is org-planner-specific and conflicts with this Relay mission's boundaries, forcing worker-level deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file states org-planner paths/ports and mandatory agent-browser at 5173 (`/Users/andy/Documents/GitHub/org-planner/.factory/skills/implementation-worker/SKILL.md`: lines 3, 10, 18, 63-65), while mission AGENTS requires Relay paths/ports (`/Users/andy/.factory/missions/e9b0e43e-24b4-4baa-87be-b21f151a466c/AGENTS.md`: lines 5, 7-9, 15, 75-76). Handoff skillFeedback also records this deviation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Route-level validation conventions are ambiguous in practice: AGENTS says all routes should use Zod middleware, but the new global-search endpoint performs manual inline query validation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS says 'Zod validation middleware on all routes for request body/params/query' (AGENTS.md line 30), while `searchController.ts` explicitly documents inline validation (lines 21-23) and `search.ts` wires `searchRouter.get('/', controller.search)` without validation middleware (line 10).

summary: Review failed due to one blocking integration gap: the Chrome extension runtime does not consume the new `/api/extension/capture` endpoint, so the extension-specific cross-area flow is not actually wired end-to-end. Also noted a non-blocking dedupe edge case in URL normalization and two shared-state observations (skill mismatch and validation-convention drift).
